task_completion.description: Access the NextGen Connect Administrator or REST API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the NextGen Connect Web Administrator UI (Mirth Connect) or a REST API client (like Postman or curl in terminal) authenticating to https://localhost:8443/api.
The Channels list in the NextGen Connect Administrator UI or API response should display a channel specifically named 'Analyzer_Gateway'.

task_completion.description: Configure the source to listen on TCP port 6661
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Source tab for 'Analyzer_Gateway' should show the Connector Type set to 'TCP Listener' and the Local Port set to '6661'.

task_completion.description: Configure the destination to write to the specified directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Destinations tab should show a destination with Connector Type 'File Writer' and the Directory set to '/home/ga/received_results/' (or '/home/ga/received_results').

task_completion.description: Implement custom ACK logic to echo the ZID segment as a ZAK segment
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Scripts tab (e.g., Postprocessor or ACK script) or Source Response settings should show JavaScript or configuration logic that extracts the transaction ID from the incoming 'ZID' segment and constructs a custom ACK containing a 'ZAK' segment with that same ID.

task_completion.description: Deploy the channel to start listening
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dashboard in NextGen Connect should show the 'Analyzer_Gateway' channel with a status of 'Started' or 'Deployed', indicating it is actively running.

integrity.description: Agent used the actual NextGen Connect application or API
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine interaction with the Mirth Connect/NextGen Connect Java Web Start UI or legitimate REST API calls. The agent should not be mocking the UI in a drawing tool or just writing text notes.

integrity.description: Agent implemented dynamic echoing rather than hardcoding the example
integrity.visual_evidence: Check the custom ACK script/configuration. The agent must use variables or message segment references (e.g., msg['ZID']['ZID.2'].toString()) to build the ZAK segment. It should NOT hardcode the example 'tx-123' from the prompt.

integrity.description: Agent did not bypass NextGen Connect with a standalone script
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not just write a Python, Node.js, or Bash script (e.g., using netcat or sockets) to listen on port 6661 and write files. The configuration must be done within NextGen Connect.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified requirements: Channel Name must be exactly 'Analyzer_Gateway'. The listening port must be 6661. The output directory must be '/home/ga/received_results'. The custom ACK must dynamically echo the transaction ID from the incoming ZID segment into a ZAK segment; hardcoding the example 'tx-123' is a failure of the dynamic requirement.
